subject: Support TOSCA functions in Node Filters
commit_message:
Support TOSCA functions in Node Filters

Adds support to use tosca functions as value in the node property
filters and substitution filters

Change-Id: Id242691cc9ddd233245b58f052b9f0e2c7bbd66b
Issue-ID: SDC-4128
Signed-off-by: André Schmid <andre.schmid@est.tech>

havent looked at ui yet, or completed testing, but comments so far

Patch Set 13:

(10 comments)

> Patch Set 11:
> 
> > Patch Set 11:
> > 
> > Some initial comments:
> >  - For the radio buttons change "Static" to "Value" and "Tosca Function" to "Value Expression"
> >  - For subsititution filters, change the label "nodefilter property" to "Property"
> >  - Is there a way to clear the value
> 
> - Add label "Operator" above box for the operator (>, <, = etc)

- For the radio buttons change "Static" to "Value" and "Tosca Function" to "Value Expression": DONE
- For subsititution filters, change the label "nodefilter property" to "Property": you mean the <Node instance name|Service name> property? I could not think in a quick way to differentiate a node filter to a substitution filter, unless I add a new input parameter to the component. Do you want to proceed or leave as it is? I could also take a better look in a separate change.
- Is there a way to clear the value: no, currently there is no way to clear the value, unless when you change from Value to Value Expression and vice-versa.
- Add label "Operator" above box for the operator (>, <, = etc): DONE

I've added a few more unit tests.
